_notes: coverage=polled  -> the site sits in a utility whose outage feed we scrape; it can be tested.
coverage=context-only -> we plot it, but its outages never appear in our data (different utility).
status=control -> matched growth corridor with NO data center. If controls run as hot as real sites, the signal is 'construction corridor', not 'data center'.
verify=true -> coordinates are town/parcel-approximate. Pull the real parcel from county GIS before trusting proximity distances.

sites.name: SoftBank/Stargate Lordstown (former GM/Foxconn plant)
sites.source: Foxconn->SoftBank sale announced Aug 2025 ($375M, $3B planned investment); Stargate AI data center photographed under construction Oct 2025 (Bloomberg). Former Lordstown Assembly, 6.2M sqft.

sites.name: Bristolville 25 - Lordstown 133-acre campus
sites.source: $3.6B, 1.65M sqft on 133 acres near former GM site. Village moratorium expires July 4 2026; developer mandamus pending at Ohio Supreme Court; Sept 2026 construction target per filings.

sites.name: Meta Bowling Green
sites.source: Announced April 2025; FE Q1 2025 investor materials place it in Toledo Edison territory.

sites.name: Perry Township (Stark Co.) 100-acre project
sites.source: 100 acres farmland; paused pending Ohio EPA / utility conditions (Ideastream, Feb 2026).

sites.name: Ravenna proposed site (Portage Co.)
sites.source: Proposed across from UH Portage Medical Center; city passed 1-year moratorium April 20 2026.

sites.name: Homer City Energy Campus
sites.source: 3,200-acre former coal plant; up to 4.5GW on-site gas; interconnects with FirstEnergy PA (Penelec) per Utility Dive Apr 2025; power production targeted 2027; PA DEP air permit Nov 2025. NOTE: largely self-powered - distribution impact is construction-phase + interconnection work.

sites.name: Upper Burrell project (Westmoreland Co.)
sites.source: One of five western-PA gas-powered DC projects (TribLive, Mar 2026). Confirm territory + parcel.

sites.name: H5 Cleveland Technology Center
sites.source: 1625 Rockwell Ave; 351k sqft, 10MW, carrier hotel

sites.name: DataBank CLE1
sites.source: 1255 Euclid Ave; 7.4k sqft raised floor, 0.4MW

sites.name: Expedient Independence
sites.source: 7500 E Pleasant Valley Rd; 37k sqft, 2MW

sites.name: Involta/colo Akron (120 N Broadway)
sites.source: 120 N Broadway St, Akron; verify operator via datacentermap

sites.name: Shippingport / Bruce Mansfield redevelopment (Beaver Co.)
sites.source: One of five western-PA gas-powered DC projects (TribLive roundup, Mar 2026). Former Bruce Mansfield coal plant site. Confirm developer + territory via Beaver Co. records.

sites.name: Springdale / former Cheswick plant site (Allegheny Co.)
sites.source: Western-PA gas-powered DC project roundup (TribLive, Mar 2026). Duquesne territory means its outages won't appear on FE's PA map; site kept for corridor context.

sites.name: Meta New Albany campus
sites.source: Operating since 2022 with continuous multi-building expansion phases underway; parcel via Licking/Franklin Co GIS

sites.name: AWS New Albany / Jug Street corridor
sites.source: Part of AWS's multibillion-dollar central-Ohio expansion; multiple parcels — treat as corridor anchor, refine per-building from county GIS

sites.name: Google New Albany
sites.source: Announced 2023-2024 New Albany build alongside existing Columbus/Lancaster sites; verify parcel

sites.name: Microsoft Licking Co. sites (New Albany/Heath/Hebron)
sites.source: Three-site $4.2B plan announced 2023; construction PAUSED on Licking Co. sites announced 2025 — status contested/paused is analytically interesting: watch for outage-rate change at pause

sites.name: QTS New Albany campus
sites.source: Large multi-building campus in the International Business Park; verify parcel via New Albany planning docs

sites.name: AWS Salem Twp (Susquehanna nuclear campus, Luzerne Co.)
sites.source: Anchor of Amazon's $20B PA commitment (Shapiro, Jun 2025). AWS bought Talen's DC + ~1,200 acres; 15 buildings planned. Co-located at Susquehanna Steam Electric Station. NOW POLLED via PA-PPL.

sites.name: AWS Falls Twp / Keystone Trade Center (Bucks Co.)
sites.source: Second anchor of the $20B AWS PA plan; former US Steel Fairless Works, 1,800-acre NorthPoint redevelopment. Draws fully from the grid. PECO territory — context only.

sites.name: AWS Kline Twp campus (Schuylkill Co.)
sites.territory: Met-Ed / PPL border — VERIFY which side (both now polled)
sites.source: DCD May 2026: 2.5M sq ft, 1,600 acres rezoned 'Special Data Center Overlay District', 15 buildings over a decade; Banks Twp also eyed. Territory must be confirmed — if Met-Ed, this is a live FE test site.

sites.name: QTS Salem Twp land assembly (Luzerne Co.)
sites.territory: PPL / Penelec border — VERIFY (both now polled)
sites.source: Fox56 Mar 2026: major land deal closed, township supervisors approved; construction could begin fall 2026. Watch for groundbreaking — a clean before/after natural experiment if it lands in Penelec.

sites.name: Quantum Frederick (former Alcoa Eastalco, Frederick Co.)
sites.source: FE newsroom: Potomac Edison building a NEW SUBSTATION to serve this campus (PJM + MD PSC review). 2,100-acre master-planned DC campus on the old Alcoa smelter site, fiber-ringed to Ashburn VA via QLoop. THE flagship FE-territory test case outside Ohio — utility-confirmed, mid-construction, grid work in progress.

sites.name: Fort Martin gas plant + DC load (Maidsville, Monongalia Co.)
sites.source: FE PR Feb 2026: 35-acre site next to Fort Martin Power Station for a new 1,200MW gas plant; PSC approval pending, site work as early as 2027. FE's own IRP says DC growth 'could change this forecast drastically'. Generation-side, not a DC itself — flagged as the WV load anchor to watch.

sites.name: TeraWulf Lake Mariner (Barker, Niagara Co.)
sites.source: Flagship NY AI campus on the retired Somerset coal plant site, 180 acres. 60MW live for Core42; 440MW leased (Fluidstack 200MW+, Google-backed); ~500MW near-term, 750MW-1GW build-out pending NYISO approval. Buildings CB-3..CB-5 delivering through 2026 — ACTIVE construction. Reuses the plant's dual 345kV lines rather than new HV build. TeraWulf 10-Q FY2026 + Schneider $290M buildout (May 2026).

sites.name: Micron Clay megafab (Onondaga Co.)
sites.source: Not a data center — a chip fab — but the largest new load in upstate NY and the same grid-stress question. Included as a load anchor; exclude from DC evidence rings if you want a clean test.

sites.name: TeraWulf western NY expansion site (TBD)
sites.source: Technology Magazine May 2026: 'lessons learned at Barker informing upcoming sites in Kentucky, western New York and Texas.' Placeholder — replace coords when the parcel is named.

sites.name: Monarch Compute Campus / Mountaineer GigaSystem (Point Pleasant, Mason Co.)
sites.source: 2,380-acre AI campus + microgrid N of Point Pleasant. Nscale acquired Mar 2026; Microsoft MOU for 1.35GW (NVIDIA Vera Rubin). Biomass/H2 permit surrendered May 2026 — pivoted to 'immediate establishment of advanced computing data centers'. NOTE: designed as a STANDALONE MICROGRID ('will not pull any power from the grid') — so steady-state load impact ~0, but 5,500 construction jobs and a 23-mile gas line = heavy construction-phase activity. Exactly the mechanism we test.

sites.name: Northern Kentucky / BE NKY hyperscale recruitment corridor (Boone/Kenton Co.)
sites.source: BE NKY actively recruiting hyperscalers near CVG airport + fiber routes; Kenton Co. among KY counties with active feasibility studies (Kentucky Lantern, May 2026: ~30 KY projects in discussion). PLACEHOLDER CENTROID — replace with the parcel when a site is named.

sites.name: CONTROL — Medina Co. growth corridor (I-71/SR-18)
sites.source: Exurban growth corridor, active residential/commercial construction, NO data center. Matched to Lordstown/Ravenna county type.

sites.name: CONTROL — Delaware Co. growth corridor (US-23/Sunbury)
sites.source: Fastest-growing OH county, heavy construction, NO data center. Control for the New Albany alley.

sites.name: CONTROL — Butler Co. growth corridor (Liberty Twp)
sites.source: Exurban Cincinnati growth, NO data center. Control for the Duke feed.

sites.name: CONTROL — Wood Co. corridor (Perrysburg)
sites.source: Growth + industrial construction in the same county as Meta Bowling Green, but ~20km away. Tightest control we have.

sites.name: Meta Jeffersonville (River Ridge Commerce Center, Clark Co. IN)
sites.source: $800M, 700k sqft, opens late 2026 — Duke Indiana's ONLY large DC per Citizens Action Coalition. RECEIPTS: Duke is shifting $216M of network upgrades for this site onto all Duke IN ratepayers (IURC Cause No. 45647 TDSIC-5), and Meta got an IURC-approved carve-out to buy carbon-free power outside Duke's system. Construction active NOW = live test window.

sites.name: Plainfield / Indianapolis-west colo corridor (Hendricks Co. IN)
sites.source: The corridor Duke cites in its ~1.5GW combined-cycle gas filing 'citing data center demand around the Indianapolis-Plainfield corridor' (IURC decision expected mid-2026). Corridor centroid — refine per-parcel.

sites.name: CONTROL — Greenwood/Johnson Co. IN growth corridor
sites.source: Fast-growing Indy suburb with heavy construction, NO data center. Control for the DEI feed.

sites.name: Meta Lebanon LEAP campus (Boone Co. IN)
sites.territory: Boone REMC / LEAP district — NOT Duke (context)
sites.source: $10B+, 1GW, broke ground 2026 — Indiana's biggest build, but NOT in Duke territory (Boone REMC community fund per Meta's own announcement). Context-only: its outages won't appear in our DEI feed.

sites.name: Amazon New Carlisle 'Project Rainier' (St. Joseph Co. IN)
sites.source: $11B, 1,200 acres. I&M shifting $400M+ of connection costs onto ratepayers (Citizens Action). Context-only for now — becomes testable if we ever add I&M's iFactor feed (same AEP family as OH-AEP).

sites.name: Data Center Alley core (Ashburn/Loudoun Co. VA)
sites.source: Largest DC concentration on Earth (~25GW+ pipeline). Context anchor for the Burden tab story; powerlines flag pulls OSM transmission around it so the corridor renders. Becomes testable if/when we add a Dominion feed.

sites.name: PW Digital Gateway (Prince William Co. VA)
sites.source: 2,100-acre corridor next to Manassas battlefield; litigation ongoing. Context anchor #2 for Alley transmission rendering.

sites.name: AWS US-East-1 Sterling/Ashburn core (Loudoun)
sites.territory: Dominion / NOVEC split — VERIFY per parcel
sites.source: The original availability zone cluster; continuous infill construction. Loudoun parcels split between Dominion and NOVEC — the split is load-bearing for evidence.

sites.name: QTS/CloudHQ Manassas corridor (Prince William)
sites.source: Innovation Park + Gainesville build wave; multiple active campuses. PW Digital Gateway litigation adjacent.

sites.name: Microsoft Boydton (Mecklenburg Co. VA)
sites.source: One of Microsoft's largest global campuses, southern VA — inside VA-DOM bbox, far from the Alley: useful non-Alley Dominion test point.

sites.name: CONTROL — Stafford Co. VA growth corridor
sites.source: I-95 exurban growth, heavy construction, no DC campus. Control for the Alley.

sites.name: Apple Maiden (Catawba Co. NC)
sites.source: Apple's flagship East campus; periodic expansion phases.

sites.name: Google Lenoir (Caldwell Co. NC)
sites.source: Long-running campus with announced expansions.

sites.name: Microsoft Catawba Co. sites (Conover/Maiden)
sites.source: Announced NC datacenter region buildout; parcel-verify.

sites.name: CONTROL — Cabarrus Co. NC growth corridor (Concord)
sites.source: Charlotte-exurb growth, no hyperscale campus. DEC control.

sites.name: Meta Forest City (Rutherford Co. NC)
sites.source: Feed live via NC-DEP.

sites.name: Google Fort Wayne (Allen Co. IN)
sites.source: $2B campus; I&M territory. Becomes testable if I&M's iFactor feed is added (same AEP family as OH-AEP).

sites.name: Sabey Indianapolis (Decatur Twp, Marion Co. IN)
sites.source: $4B campus announcement; AES Indiana territory (separate from our AES Ohio feed).

sites.name: Google Monrovia (Morgan Co. IN)
sites.source: ~1,200MW announced campus SW of Indianapolis.

sites.name: Microsoft La Porte/Granger (northern IN)
sites.source: Northern-Indiana proposals in the New Carlisle corridor wave.

sites.name: TeraWulf 'Muskie' EastPark (Boyd Co. KY)
sites.source: Lake Mariner lessons applied to Kentucky; AEP-family feed would make it testable.

sites.name: Google Douglas Co. (Lithia Springs GA)
sites.source: Long-running campus west of Atlanta with announced expansion phases.

sites.name: Meta Newton Co. (Social Circle GA)
sites.source: Multi-building campus east of Atlanta; continuing build-out.

sites.name: QTS Atlanta-Metro (Fulton Co. GA)
sites.source: One of the largest US colo campuses; on-site substation capacity.

sites.name: Atlanta metro hyperscale wave (contextual anchor)
sites.source: GPC's IRP updates cite multi-GW DC pipeline; replace with named parcels as they firm up.

sites.source: Fast-growing Atlanta exurb, heavy construction, no DC campus.
